_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of component and interface specifications
reference_document_element_description: Ability to design data product interfaces that serve diverse consumer needs across different technical proficiency levels without creating unsustainable maintenance burden.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. Ability DQ-A-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-014 (DQ, cosine: 0.496), DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.463).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.2/10 (raw 0.325). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [component, interface, knowledge, specifications] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, across, burden, consumer, creating, data] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.860 (P=0.882, R=0.838). P exceeds R by 0.044 — the NICE element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of component and interface specifications" and "Ability to design data product interfaces that serve diverse consumer needs acro" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.507 > STS 0.325 (gap +0.182). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [component, interface, knowledge, specifications]. WIDAI terms: [ability, across, burden, consumer, creating, data].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.182 with low STS 0.325. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
